content.text: I'll work through this methodically. Let me start by understanding the telemetry source code and f

content.content: File content (13734 tokens) exceeds maximum allowed tokens (10000). Use offset and limit parameters to read specific portions of the file, or search for specific content instead of reading the whole f

content.text: Let me read the aggregator and find good sessions.

input.command: for f in $(ls ~/.claude/data/sessions/*.md | tail -50); do size=$(wc -l < "$f"); echo "$size $f"; done | sort -rn | head -20
input.description: Find largest session files by line count

content.text: The session `.md` files are small -- they're metadata, not transcripts. I need to find the JSONL fil

input.description: List JSONL files in construct project

input.command: for f in ~/.claude/projects/-home-testuser-construct/*.jsonl; do lines=$(wc -l < "$f"); size=$(stat -c%s "$f"); echo "$lines $size $(basename $f)"; done | sort -rn | head -20
input.description: Sort JSONL files by line count

content.text: Good. Let me look at a few of these to find sessions with diverse tool usage and subagents. I'll sam

content.text: These are all very large sessions (hundreds of user messages). For e2e tests, I need smaller, manage

input.command: for f in ~/.claude/projects/-home-testuser-construct/*.jsonl; do lines=$(wc -l < "$f"); echo "$lines $(basename $f)"; done | sort -n | awk '$1 >= 30 && $1 <= 200' | head -30

input.description: Analyze mid-sized sessions for tool diversity

content.text: Good candidates, but they're a bit small on tool diversity. Let me also check other projects and loo

input.command: for f in ~/.claude/projects/-home-testuser-construct/*.jsonl; do lines=$(wc -l < "$f"); echo "$lines $(basename $f)"; done | sort -n | awk '$1 >= 200 && $1 <= 500'

content.text: Good selections. Let me also check other projects for more variety and look for sessions with compac

input.command: for proj in -home-testuser -home-testuser-edm -home-testuser-budget -home-testuser-goals -home-testuser-voice-caller; do echo "=== $proj ==="; ls ~/.claude/projects/$proj/*.jsonl 2>/dev/null | wc -l; done
for f in ~/.claude/projects/-home-testuser/*.jsonl; do lines=$(wc -l < "$f"); echo "$lines $(basename $f)"; done | sort -n | awk '$1 >= 50 && $1 <= 300' | head -15
input.description: Find mid-sized sessions in home project

content.text: OK, I have enough context. Let me select 5 diverse sessions. I'll pick these to get good coverage:

input.command: for f in ~/.claude/projects/-home-testuser-construct/*.jsonl; do lines=$(wc -l < "$f"); echo "$lines $(basename $f)"; done | sort -n | head -15
